title: US-European defense tensions escalate as Trump threatens troop withdrawals from NATO allies
summary: Transatlantic tensions reached new heights as Trump threatened to pull US troops from Germany, Italy, and Spain amid disagreements over the Iran war, with a German MEP stating 'the trust in this White House is not the same.'
contents:
## Transatlantic Defense Tensions Escalate

### Trump's Threat
President Trump threatened to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain, extending similar warnings already made against Germany. The threats came amid European reluctance to support US-Israeli operations against Iran, including in the Strait of Hormuz.

### European Response
German Member of European Parliament Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann told CNN that Germans have lost trust in US security commitments. "The trust in this White House differs from the trust under Biden, under Obama, under Clinton," she stated directly when asked about German confidence in US protection.

### Nordic-Baltic Impact
The tension manifests concretely in delayed weapons deliveries to Nordic and Baltic states. Norway, Finland, and Estonia have all acknowledged delays in US defense deliveries due to the Middle East war's strain on American stockpiles, undermining NATO's northern flank readiness.

### Coalition Building Difficulties
The US pressed foreign governments to join a new "Maritime Freedom Construct" coalition to support freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. France, Britain, and other countries conditioned their participation on an end to the conflict, refusing to contribute while hostilities continue.

### Strategic Assessment
The rift between Washington and European capitals represents the most significant strain on NATO cohesion since the alliance's founding. European nations increasingly discuss greater defense independence, accelerating investment in indigenous capabilities while questioning the reliability of US security guarantees.
